markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' repeated delays for Grand Theft Auto VI, most recently shifting the release to November 19, 2026, for additional polish, have extended the window for intervening events and reshaped trader focus on this speculative market. Development challenges, including staff adjustments and internal pressures reported in late 2025, underscore the studio's emphasis on quality over speed, consistent with the franchise's history of extended cycles since GTA V's 2013 launch and ongoing GTA Online support. Traders monitor music releases, tech milestones, and geopolitical shifts as potential resolutions, while industry dynamics like Take-Two Interactive's fiscal priorities and Rockstar's secrecy around marketing campaigns add layers of uncertainty. The November date now serves as the key near-term catalyst, with any further slippage likely amplifying volatility in outcomes.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The market's heavy weighting toward no additional prison time reflects Harvey Weinstein's ongoing appeals of his 16-year California conviction and the May 2026 mistrial in his third New York rape trial, where jurors deadlocked 9-3 in favor of acquittal on the remaining charge. Prosecutors have 30 days to decide on a potential fourth trial, while Weinstein, now 74 and battling cancer, diabetes, and other health issues, continues serving time at Rikers Island under consecutive sentencing rules. Recent mixed verdicts and repeated hung juries have eroded momentum for further convictions, leaving the total time served dependent on appeal outcomes and any plea considerations rather than new lengthy sentences.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights hold a 2-1 series lead in the 2026 Stanley Cup Final against the Carolina Hurricanes, driving their 61.5% implied probability as traders price in home-ice advantage and recent overtime resilience. Carolina posted the NHL's best regular-season record at 53-22-7 with 113 points, yet Vegas overcame a lower 95-point total through strong playoff execution, including a double-overtime Game 3 victory. Momentum shifts have defined the matchup, with Vegas stealing Game 1 on the road before Carolina responded in Game 2. Official injury reports and lineup stability remain key variables heading into Game 4, while historical patterns favor teams that secure early series leads in best-of-seven formats.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion

eventMetadata.context_description: The Knicks hold a 2-1 lead in the 2026 NBA Finals after road wins in Games 1 and 2 followed by a narrow home loss in Game 3, where the Spurs mounted a second-half rally. The best-of-seven format leaves room for further swings based on adjustments and execution.

The Spurs, the second-seeded West squad reaching the title round for the first time since 2014, keep implied probability alive through Victor Wembanyama's impact and recent momentum despite the deficit. The best-of-seven format leaves room for further swings based on adjustments and execution.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit as near co-favorites in the World Cup winner market because both enter with elite squad depth, recent major-tournament success, and favorable group-stage paths. Spain’s young core, anchored by Lamine Yamal and strengthened by Euro 2024 form, edges models in some projections, while France counters with Kylian Mbappé’s leadership and proven knockout resilience. England, Argentina, Brazil, and Portugal follow closely on the strength of consistent international results, experienced attacking options, and solid qualifying campaigns that wrapped in March. The narrow spread among the top five reflects a deep field where any of several sides can reach the later stages, with minor roster tweaks or early results capable of shifting implied probabilities ahead of the June kickoff.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit as near co-favorites in the World Cup winner market because both enter with elite squad depth, recent major-tournament success, and favorable group-stage paths. Spain’s young core, anchored by Lamine Yamal and strengthened by Euro 2024 form, edges models in some projections, while France counters with Kylian Mbappé’s leadership and proven knockout resilience. England, Argentina, Brazil, and Portugal follow closely on the strength of consistent international results, experienced attacking options, and solid qualifying campaigns that wrapped in March. The narrow spread among the top five reflects a deep field where any of several sides can reach the later stages, with minor roster tweaks or early results capable of shifting implied probabilities ahead of the June kickoff.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, with no verified challenges to his leadership emerging in 2025 or early 2026. Official proceedings, including the rollout of the 15th Five-Year Plan and New Year addresses, have emphasized continuity under his direction ahead of the 21st Party Congress in 2027. Military leadership changes have aligned with established patterns of centralized authority rather than signaling instability. Analyses of elite dynamics show the Politburo and Standing Committee remain dominated by his allies, with discussion focused on a potential fourth term rather than transition. Trader consensus at 92% for continued tenure through 2026 reflects the absence of institutional or factional pressures capable of altering this trajectory before the Congress.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the fragmented 2028 Democratic presidential nominee market at 23.8% amid early positioning by governors and members of Congress, reflecting name recognition and his California executive record ahead of any formal announcements. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 8.9% as she expands national visibility through recent events and outreach, while figures like Jon Ossoff and Kamala Harris trail with lower shares tied to Senate or prior national roles. Traders price in a wide-open field shaped by post-2024 dynamics, with key differentiators including governing experience versus legislative profiles and regional voter outreach efforts in the South. Consolidation could accelerate after 2026 midterm results or through early endorsements and polling shifts among potential primary voters.
